1	# Risk Rules — Policy Doc
2	
3	**Last updated:** 2026-04-18 (meta-integration additions — §1.B, §4.B, §5.A, §8)
4	**Status:** Active. Binding on every trade.
5	
6	This file promotes the sizing, heat, and stop rules from `framework/Trad core.md` and `framework/Coin core.md` into explicit policy. Reference at every entry. When a rule here conflicts with research prose in the cores, this file wins.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## 1. Position Sizing
11	
12	- **Method:** Inverse-ATR volatility targeting with fractional-Kelly overlay.
13	  - Position $ = (Target Risk $) / (ATR × Multiplier).
14	  - ATR multiplier: 2–3× for trending assets (commodities, crypto); 1.5–2× for equities.
15	  - Source: `framework/Trad core.md:203`.
16	- **Kelly fraction:** **Quarter-Kelly**, maximum 25% single-position cap.
17	  - Rationale: Full Kelly produces 40–60%+ drawdowns; quarter-Kelly is the professional consensus for crypto and is the right regime for current elevated VIX. Source: `framework/Trad core.md:205`, `framework/Coin core.md:53`.
18	
19	### 1.B Intermediary-capital-aware sizing (V027 as primary sizer — added 2026-04-18)
20	
21	V027 (NY Fed primary-dealer capital z-score) is the primary cross-asset sizer above the inverse-ATR / quarter-Kelly rules. Apply this tier first, then take the more restrictive of V027 and the standard sizer.
22	
23	| V027 z-score regime | Sizing action | Scope |
24	|---|---|---|
25	| z > +0.5σ (capital expansion) | Full inverse-ATR sizing on V009 + V028 spine | All risk-asset sleeves |
26	| −1σ ≤ z ≤ +0.5σ (neutral) | Standard inverse-ATR sizing (no V027 adjustment) | All risk-asset sleeves |
27	| z < −1σ (capital contraction) | **Halve gross exposure** on all risk-asset sleeves | Equities, commodities, FX longs, crypto |
28	
29	This sits *above* the quarter-Kelly cap. Take the more restrictive of the two. See also the Step 5 double-counting gate with V004 HY OAS in `framework/Methodology Prompt.md §4 Reconciliation note 2026-04-18`.
30	
31	## 2. Risk per Trade and Portfolio Heat
32	
33	- **Single position risk at stop:** 0.5–2% of portfolio.
34	  - 0.5% for illiquid names: PLTR, palladium, platinum. Source: `framework/Trad core.md:210`.
35	  - 1–2% for liquid names.
36	- **Total portfolio heat:** **6–8%** max (sum of all live position risks to stop). Source: `framework/Trad core.md:209`.
37	- **Sector cap:** 25% max any single sector. Source: `framework/Trad core.md:211`.
38	- **Crypto cap:** 5–15% of portfolio. Source: `framework/Trad core.md:211`.
39	
40	## 3. Stops and Exits
41	
42	- **Stops:** ATR-based trailing at 2–3× ATR. Not fixed-percentage.
43	  - Evidence: 2–3× ATR reduces momentum strategy max DD from −49.8% to −11.4% (Han et al. 2016, via `framework/Trad core.md:213`).
44	- **TP structure (crypto baseline):** TP1 at 1.5× ATR, TP2 at 3× ATR; at TP1 hit, move stop to breakeven. Source: `framework/Coin core.md:57`.
45	- **Chandelier exit:** 3× ATR below highest high for crypto trend follows. Source: `framework/Coin core.md:57`.
46	- **Time-to-invalidation:** Every thesis must also carry a date by which the setup must have moved in favor. A 4-week thesis that hasn't moved in 4 weeks is a failed thesis. Close and free the capital.
47	- **Intraday stop tightening:** If a protective stop is moved intraday to a level tighter than the methodology stop (2–3× ATR), document the reason in `framework/Memory.md §2` and confirm that position size is calibrated to the tighter stop, not the methodology stop. An undocumented intraday tightening that trips removes the edge of the signal without a sizing credit. Rule of thumb: keep the methodology stop OR tighten the stop AND halve the size — never tighten without a documented reason. (Added 2026-04-20; triggered by EWY 2026-04-16 lesson.)
48	
49	## 4. Drawdown Circuit Breakers
50	
51	- At **−15% portfolio DD:** reduce gross exposure to 50%.
52	- At **−20% portfolio DD:** move to defensive (gold + cash overweight; no new risk-on entries).
53	- Source: `framework/Trad core.md:214`.
54	
55	### 4.B Overlay drawdown gate (C009 Faber TAA — added 2026-04-18)
56	
57	V033–V035 C009 Faber 10-month SMA is a per-sleeve drawdown circuit-breaker, independent of the −15% / −20% portfolio-level breakers above. Both gates apply; take the more restrictive.
58	
59	- **SPY/QQQ below 10m-SMA** at previous month-end close → equity-sleeve gross to zero (no new longs). V009 + V028 continue to size inside the remaining non-gated sleeves.
60	- **GSCI (commodity aggregate) below 10m-SMA** → commodity sleeve to zero.
61	- **BTC-USD below 10m-SMA** → crypto sleeve to zero.
62	- **EFA (optional, international equity) below 10m-SMA** → EWJ/EWY sleeve to zero.
63	
64	**Read cadence.** Overlay state flips only at end-of-month. Daily pipeline reads the last month-end close vs the 10m-SMA; intraday recompute is prohibited.
65	
66	**Gate interaction.** If the portfolio is already at the −15% breaker and a sleeve is simultaneously gated off, the more restrictive rule wins — in practice both apply additively (reduce gross AND zero the gated sleeve). Log the binding constraint in `framework/Memory.md §2` Notes.
67	
68	## 5. Correlation / Concentration Gate (Step 6 overlay)
69	
70	Before accepting a |sum|≥3 signal as a fresh trade, check:
71	- Does this thesis share its primary regime variable with any open position? (e.g., Copper long + Gold long + Silver long = one reflation/DXY-weak bet, not three.)
72	- If yes: treat as an **add** to the existing theme and size the combined bet to the sector cap, not the per-position cap.
73	- BTC and ETH momentum fire simultaneously ~80% of the time (`framework/Trad core.md:199`) — never double-count these as independent.
74	
75	### 5.A R-group scale-consistency (added 2026-04-18)
76	
77	When multiple R-inputs (Step 5 risk overlay variables) flag the same stress on the same asset, **do not sum their contributions — count only the single most-negative input**:
78	
79	- Post-2026-04-18 meta downgrade (pending Gerald approval at Phase 1 diff review): V001 VIX and V004 HY OAS are Grade B, retained for monitoring but not additively scored alongside V027.
80	- Primary R input is V027 (intermediary capital z-score). If V027 flags stress, V004 and V001 do not add to the score even if they also flag.
81	- If V027 is neutral but V004 and V001 both flag, take the more-negative of V004 / V001 for the R-score (single value, not their sum).
82	- V030 DealerGamma short-gamma regime also participates in this rule — when co-firing with V001, take the more-negative.
83	
84	## 6. Rebalancing Cadence
85	
86	- **Hybrid threshold rebalance:** monitor daily, rebalance when 5% absolute drift or a signal change. Tighter 3% bands for crypto.
87	- Source: `framework/Trad core.md:218`.
88	
89	## 7. Pre-Entry Checklist (use every trade)
90	
91	1. Methodology aggregate |sum| ≥ 3? (`framework/Methodology Prompt.md:73`)
92	2. Invalidation written, concrete, date-bounded? (`framework/Methodology Prompt.md:87`)
93	3. Correlation gate clean (Section 5 above)?
94	4. Per-position risk ≤ 2% AND portfolio heat post-entry ≤ 8%?
95	5. ATR stop set, not fixed %? If stop tightened intraday, size re-calibrated to the tighter stop and reason documented?
96	6. Catalyst scored and aligned (surprise vs confirmation dependency stated)?
97	7. **Step 1.5 Overlay Gate status for this sleeve is ON?** (added 2026-04-18; if OFF, position size × 0 — no trade regardless of Sum)
98	8. **V027 intermediary-capital sizing tier applied?** (added 2026-04-18; if z < −1σ, the halved gross sizing is the binding constraint)
99	
100	If any answer is no: no trade.
101	
102	## 8. Factor Sleeves (added 2026-04-18, meta-integration)
103	
104	V029 BAB and V030 DealerGamma run as **independent factor sleeves**, separate from the V009 / V027 / V028 spine.
105	
106	- **Each sleeve capped at 1/3 of V009's risk budget.**
107	- Do NOT aggregate sleeve weights into the spine sizing.
108	- Correlation gate still applies: if a BAB sleeve leg and a V009 spine long are on the same ticker, size to the combined sector cap, not double-sized.
109	- V030 DealerGamma is Grade B (single-paper, pending second replication). Review status at 2026-07-01 quarterly and at every trade rec referencing it.
110	- Sleeve weights, when active on a given day, are logged to the SignalLedger under `bab_sleeve_weight` and `dealergamma_sleeve_weight` (schema delta proposed at Phase 1 Gate 1 — see deployment memo for approval status).

# Risk Rules — Policy Doc

**Last updated:** 2026-04-18 (meta-integration additions — §1.B, §4.B, §5.A, §8)
**Status:** Active. Binding on every trade.

This file promotes the sizing, heat, and stop rules from `framework/Trad core.md` and `framework/Coin core.md` into explicit policy. Reference at every entry. When a rule here conflicts with research prose in the cores, this file wins.

---

## 1. Position Sizing

- **Method:** Inverse-ATR volatility targeting with fractional-Kelly overlay.
  - Position $ = (Target Risk $) / (ATR × Multiplier).
  - ATR multiplier: 2–3× for trending assets (commodities, crypto); 1.5–2× for equities.
  - Source: `framework/Trad core.md:203`.
- **Kelly fraction:** **Quarter-Kelly**, maximum 25% single-position cap.
  - Rationale: Full Kelly produces 40–60%+ drawdowns; quarter-Kelly is the professional consensus for crypto and is the right regime for current elevated VIX. Source: `framework/Trad core.md:205`, `framework/Coin core.md:53`.

### 1.B Intermediary-capital-aware sizing (V027 as primary sizer — added 2026-04-18)

V027 (NY Fed primary-dealer capital z-score) is the primary cross-asset sizer above the inverse-ATR / quarter-Kelly rules. Apply this tier first, then take the more restrictive of V027 and the standard sizer.

| V027 z-score regime | Sizing action | Scope |
|---|---|---|
| z > +0.5σ (capital expansion) | Full inverse-ATR sizing on V009 + V028 spine | All risk-asset sleeves |
| −1σ ≤ z ≤ +0.5σ (neutral) | Standard inverse-ATR sizing (no V027 adjustment) | All risk-asset sleeves |
| z < −1σ (capital contraction) | **Halve gross exposure** on all risk-asset sleeves | Equities, commodities, FX longs, crypto |

This sits *above* the quarter-Kelly cap. Take the more restrictive of the two. See also the Step 5 double-counting gate with V004 HY OAS in `framework/Methodology Prompt.md §4 Reconciliation note 2026-04-18`.

## 2. Risk per Trade and Portfolio Heat

- **Single position risk at stop:** 0.5–2% of portfolio.
  - 0.5% for illiquid names: PLTR, palladium, platinum. Source: `framework/Trad core.md:210`.
  - 1–2% for liquid names.
- **Total portfolio heat:** **6–8%** max (sum of all live position risks to stop). Source: `framework/Trad core.md:209`.
- **Sector cap:** 25% max any single sector. Source: `framework/Trad core.md:211`.
- **Crypto cap:** 5–15% of portfolio. Source: `framework/Trad core.md:211`.

## 3. Stops and Exits

- **Stops:** ATR-based trailing at 2–3× ATR. Not fixed-percentage.
  - Evidence: 2–3× ATR reduces momentum strategy max DD from −49.8% to −11.4% (Han et al. 2016, via `framework/Trad core.md:213`).
- **TP structure (crypto baseline):** TP1 at 1.5× ATR, TP2 at 3× ATR; at TP1 hit, move stop to breakeven. Source: `framework/Coin core.md:57`.
- **Chandelier exit:** 3× ATR below highest high for crypto trend follows. Source: `framework/Coin core.md:57`.
- **Time-to-invalidation:** Every thesis must also carry a date by which the setup must have moved in favor. A 4-week thesis that hasn't moved in 4 weeks is a failed thesis. Close and free the capital.
- **Intraday stop tightening:** If a protective stop is moved intraday to a level tighter than the methodology stop (2–3× ATR), document the reason in `framework/Memory.md §2` and confirm that position size is calibrated to the tighter stop, not the methodology stop. An undocumented intraday tightening that trips removes the edge of the signal without a sizing credit. Rule of thumb: keep the methodology stop OR tighten the stop AND halve the size — never tighten without a documented reason. (Added 2026-04-20; triggered by EWY 2026-04-16 lesson.)

## 4. Drawdown Circuit Breakers

- At **−15% portfolio DD:** reduce gross exposure to 50%.
- At **−20% portfolio DD:** move to defensive (gold + cash overweight; no new risk-on entries).
- Source: `framework/Trad core.md:214`.

### 4.B Overlay drawdown gate (C009 Faber TAA — added 2026-04-18)

V033–V035 C009 Faber 10-month SMA is a per-sleeve drawdown circuit-breaker, independent of the −15% / −20% portfolio-level breakers above. Both gates apply; take the more restrictive.

- **SPY/QQQ below 10m-SMA** at previous month-end close → equity-sleeve gross to zero (no new longs). V009 + V028 continue to size inside the remaining non-gated sleeves.
- **GSCI (commodity aggregate) below 10m-SMA** → commodity sleeve to zero.
- **BTC-USD below 10m-SMA** → crypto sleeve to zero.
- **EFA (optional, international equity) below 10m-SMA** → EWJ/EWY sleeve to zero.

**Read cadence.** Overlay state flips only at end-of-month. Daily pipeline reads the last month-end close vs the 10m-SMA; intraday recompute is prohibited.

**Gate interaction.** If the portfolio is already at the −15% breaker and a sleeve is simultaneously gated off, the more restrictive rule wins — in practice both apply additively (reduce gross AND zero the gated sleeve). Log the binding constraint in `framework/Memory.md §2` Notes.

## 5. Correlation / Concentration Gate (Step 6 overlay)

Before accepting a |sum|≥3 signal as a fresh trade, check:
- Does this thesis share its primary regime variable with any open position? (e.g., Copper long + Gold long + Silver long = one reflation/DXY-weak bet, not three.)
- If yes: treat as an **add** to the existing theme and size the combined bet to the sector cap, not the per-position cap.
- BTC and ETH momentum fire simultaneously ~80% of the time (`framework/Trad core.md:199`) — never double-count these as independent.

### 5.A R-group scale-consistency (added 2026-04-18)

When multiple R-inputs (Step 5 risk overlay variables) flag the same stress on the same asset, **do not sum their contributions — count only the single most-negative input**:

- Post-2026-04-18 meta downgrade (pending Gerald approval at Phase 1 diff review): V001 VIX and V004 HY OAS are Grade B, retained for monitoring but not additively scored alongside V027.
- Primary R input is V027 (intermediary capital z-score). If V027 flags stress, V004 and V001 do not add to the score even if they also flag.
- If V027 is neutral but V004 and V001 both flag, take the more-negative of V004 / V001 for the R-score (single value, not their sum).
- V030 DealerGamma short-gamma regime also participates in this rule — when co-firing with V001, take the more-negative.

## 6. Rebalancing Cadence

- **Hybrid threshold rebalance:** monitor daily, rebalance when 5% absolute drift or a signal change. Tighter 3% bands for crypto.
- Source: `framework/Trad core.md:218`.

## 7. Pre-Entry Checklist (use every trade)

1. Methodology aggregate |sum| ≥ 3? (`framework/Methodology Prompt.md:73`)
2. Invalidation written, concrete, date-bounded? (`framework/Methodology Prompt.md:87`)
3. Correlation gate clean (Section 5 above)?
4. Per-position risk ≤ 2% AND portfolio heat post-entry ≤ 8%?
5. ATR stop set, not fixed %? If stop tightened intraday, size re-calibrated to the tighter stop and reason documented?
6. Catalyst scored and aligned (surprise vs confirmation dependency stated)?
7. **Step 1.5 Overlay Gate status for this sleeve is ON?** (added 2026-04-18; if OFF, position size × 0 — no trade regardless of Sum)
8. **V027 intermediary-capital sizing tier applied?** (added 2026-04-18; if z < −1σ, the halved gross sizing is the binding constraint)

If any answer is no: no trade.

## 8. Factor Sleeves (added 2026-04-18, meta-integration)

V029 BAB and V030 DealerGamma run as **independent factor sleeves**, separate from the V009 / V027 / V028 spine.

- **Each sleeve capped at 1/3 of V009's risk budget.**
- Do NOT aggregate sleeve weights into the spine sizing.
- Correlation gate still applies: if a BAB sleeve leg and a V009 spine long are on the same ticker, size to the combined sector cap, not double-sized.
- V030 DealerGamma is Grade B (single-paper, pending second replication). Review status at 2026-07-01 quarterly and at every trade rec referencing it.
- Sleeve weights, when active on a given day, are logged to the SignalLedger under `bab_sleeve_weight` and `dealergamma_sleeve_weight` (schema delta proposed at Phase 1 Gate 1 — see deployment memo for approval status).

{"files":[{"contentSnippet":"\# Daily News & Events Capture — 2026-04-21  
\*\*Run time:\*\* 18:30 UTC+8 | \*\*Categories covered:\*\* 12 | \*\*Catalysts flagged:\*\* 8  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 1. MACRO RELEASES  
\*\*Grade A data available.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*Initial Jobless Claims\*\* (week ending Apr 11): 207K — below consensus, labor market resilient. Prior week (Apr 4): 219K (above prior 203K and 210K consensus), suggesting some early-April softening before this reversal.  
\- \*\*Earnings calendar\*\*: 82 S\&P 500 companies report Q1 results on Apr 21. Season-to-date (10% reported): 88% beat EPS — above 5-year avg of 78%.  
\- No PMI or GDP print scheduled for Apr 21 per BLS/BEA calendar review.  
  
\*\*Catalyst:\*\* Jobless claims trajectory (softening then recovery) reduces near-term Fed cut urgency. Watch for claims drift above 220K as labor inflection signal.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 2. CENTRAL BANK  
\*\*Grade A: official CB communications + market pricing.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*Fed\*\*: Policy rate at 3.50-3.75%. Holding steady; resilient labor + elevated oil-driven inflation reduces near-term cut probability. Geopolitical premium now embedded.  
\- \*\*ECB\*\*: February meeting held rates unchanged. April pivot signal — ECB flagged shift back to "driving at sight" approach. At least one insurance rate hike now priced in, driven by rising energy prices. Hawkish shift vs. prior easing trajectory.  
\- \*\*BOJ\*\*: April hike timing now brought forward (previously June target). Governor Ueda dovish tone aimed to signal uncertainty, not rule out April hike. 0.75% current rate; next hike likely in April or June. 160.00 USD/JPY remains line-in-sand for Finance Ministry intervention.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[HIGH\]:\*\* BOJ April hike risk -\> JPY strengthening catalyst; USD/JPY downside risk. Asset: JPY longs / USD/JPY shorts. Direction: Bearish USD/JPY. Date: BOJ meeting TBD April 2026.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 3. GEOPOLITICS  
\*\*Grade A: Reuters/CNBC/WaPo primary reporting.\*\*  
  
\#\#\# Iran-US / Strait of Hormuz — CRITICAL ACTIVE EVENT  
\- Iran \*\*closed Strait of Hormuz again\*\* (Apr 18) after US refused to lift naval blockade on Iranian ports. Prior brief reopening Apr 17 voided.  
\- Apr 19: US destroyed Iranian tanker amid ceasefire standoff; Iran vowed retaliation. Ceasefire deadline imminent.  
\- Apr 21 (today): CNBC — "Oil falls as investors assess mixed messaging on Iran peace talks ahead of ceasefire deadline." Peace talks ongoing but sticking points: Iran nuclear stockpile and Hormuz control unresolved.  
\- \*\*Supply impact (Grade A — IEA):\*\* IEA characterized as "largest supply disruption in history of global oil market." Oil production of Kuwait, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, UAE collectively down 10+ mb/d. Global supply -1.5 mb/d YoY to \~104.7 mb/d for 2026.  
  
\#\#\# Ukraine-Russia  
\- Russian offensive ongoing in Donetsk; infrastructure strikes continuing. US-mediated ceasefire talks ongoing. Durable peace probability low. 50%+ chance of further escalation per CFR 2026 risk assessment.  
  
\#\#\# Taiwan Strait  
\- US-China trade truce keeping tensions at elevated-but-stable level. Continued arms sales risk triggering aggressive Chinese military posture. Background risk, no immediate flash event.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[CRITICAL\]:\*\* Hormuz partial-to-full closure -\> oil supply shock ongoing. Asset: CL (crude oil), energy equities, airline shorts, gold. Direction: Bullish CL/energy on supply shock, bearish gold short-term (rate hike risk). Date: Active, Apr 21 through ceasefire resolution.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 4. EQUITY EARNINGS  
\*\*Grade A: FactSet Earnings Insight Apr 17, 2026.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*Q1 2026 season\*\* (10% reported): 88% EPS beat rate (5Y avg: 78%). Strong start.  
\- \*\*Magnificent 7\*\*: Estimated +22.8% YoY earnings growth for Q1. NVIDIA top contributor to S\&P 500 growth.  
\- \*\*Rest of S\&P 500 (493 cos.)\*\*: +10.1% blended growth.  
\- \*\*CY 2026 full-year consensus\*\*: +18.0% EPS growth. All 11 sectors projected to show revenue growth.  
\- \*\*Apr 21 reporters\*\*: 82 companies scheduled. Volume is above-average for a single session.  
\- \*\*Sector leadership in beats\*\*: IT, Communication Services, Financials leading revenue growth expectations.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[HIGH\]:\*\* Any Mag-7 miss vs. +22.8% bar could trigger sector drawdown. Watch for guidance cuts citing supply chain disruption from Hormuz closure.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 5. CRYPTO REGULATORY  
\*\*Grade A: SEC press release, GENIUS Act text.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*SEC-CFTC token taxonomy\*\* (Mar 17, 2026): Joint interpretation clarifying federal securities law application to cryptoassets. Token categories: digital commodities, digital collectibles, digital tools, stablecoins, digital securities.  
\- \*\*GENIUS Act\*\* (stablecoin law, signed Jul 2025): Full implementation targeted 2026. Rules finalization deadline: July 18, 2026. Interest payments to holders prohibited. Stablecoin holders granted priority in insolvency.  
\- \*\*ETF landscape\*\*...","createdTime":"2026-04-21T10:51:43.660Z","fileExtension":"md","fileSize":"13797","id":"11u0misywlUKlgGz2cyR_G_5oUmxQQXUY","mimeType":"text/plain","modifiedTime":"2026-04-21T10:51:43.660Z","owner":"gerald.yiu.12529@gmail.com","parentId":"1uaxnX3hcTdPmpMO1YEYXe9sCQNJVX0hc","title":"news-2026-04-21.md","viewUrl":"https://drive.google.com/file/d/11u0misywlUKlgGz2cyR_G_5oUmxQQXUY/view?usp=drivesdk","viewedByMeTime":"2026-04-21T10:52:46.968Z"}],"nextPageToken":"~!!~AI9FV7Sf5qbCYBlrSHQCjy5JOJ6Q-1Zpz-L6DQc3DWzZ-C6EuR53fPq-15vfcbtPMr_Yf-40RO-FtTH3ytP2Ds_SRuayGwqOtbV7r-FscpatziR4wOros2Cl3tcwd9_1O5Q-lBulyYuUDgnpqaeYJo5-n_JRyBoXzw=="}

\# Daily News & Events Capture — 2026-04-21  
\*\*Run time:\*\* 18:30 UTC+8 | \*\*Categories covered:\*\* 12 | \*\*Catalysts flagged:\*\* 8  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 1. MACRO RELEASES  
\*\*Grade A data available.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*Initial Jobless Claims\*\* (week ending Apr 11): 207K — below consensus, labor market resilient. Prior week (Apr 4): 219K (above prior 203K and 210K consensus), suggesting some early-April softening before this reversal.  
\- \*\*Earnings calendar\*\*: 82 S\&P 500 companies report Q1 results on Apr 21. Season-to-date (10% reported): 88% beat EPS — above 5-year avg of 78%.  
\- No PMI or GDP print scheduled for Apr 21 per BLS/BEA calendar review.  
  
\*\*Catalyst:\*\* Jobless claims trajectory (softening then recovery) reduces near-term Fed cut urgency. Watch for claims drift above 220K as labor inflection signal.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 2. CENTRAL BANK  
\*\*Grade A: official CB communications + market pricing.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*Fed\*\*: Policy rate at 3.50-3.75%. Holding steady; resilient labor + elevated oil-driven inflation reduces near-term cut probability. Geopolitical premium now embedded.  
\- \*\*ECB\*\*: February meeting held rates unchanged. April pivot signal — ECB flagged shift back to "driving at sight" approach. At least one insurance rate hike now priced in, driven by rising energy prices. Hawkish shift vs. prior easing trajectory.  
\- \*\*BOJ\*\*: April hike timing now brought forward (previously June target). Governor Ueda dovish tone aimed to signal uncertainty, not rule out April hike. 0.75% current rate; next hike likely in April or June. 160.00 USD/JPY remains line-in-sand for Finance Ministry intervention.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[HIGH\]:\*\* BOJ April hike risk -\> JPY strengthening catalyst; USD/JPY downside risk. Asset: JPY longs / USD/JPY shorts. Direction: Bearish USD/JPY. Date: BOJ meeting TBD April 2026.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 3. GEOPOLITICS  
\*\*Grade A: Reuters/CNBC/WaPo primary reporting.\*\*  
  
\#\#\# Iran-US / Strait of Hormuz — CRITICAL ACTIVE EVENT  
\- Iran \*\*closed Strait of Hormuz again\*\* (Apr 18) after US refused to lift naval blockade on Iranian ports. Prior brief reopening Apr 17 voided.  
\- Apr 19: US destroyed Iranian tanker amid ceasefire standoff; Iran vowed retaliation. Ceasefire deadline imminent.  
\- Apr 21 (today): CNBC — "Oil falls as investors assess mixed messaging on Iran peace talks ahead of ceasefire deadline." Peace talks ongoing but sticking points: Iran nuclear stockpile and Hormuz control unresolved.  
\- \*\*Supply impact (Grade A — IEA):\*\* IEA characterized as "largest supply disruption in history of global oil market." Oil production of Kuwait, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, UAE collectively down 10+ mb/d. Global supply -1.5 mb/d YoY to \~104.7 mb/d for 2026.  
  
\#\#\# Ukraine-Russia  
\- Russian offensive ongoing in Donetsk; infrastructure strikes continuing. US-mediated ceasefire talks ongoing. Durable peace probability low. 50%+ chance of further escalation per CFR 2026 risk assessment.  
  
\#\#\# Taiwan Strait  
\- US-China trade truce keeping tensions at elevated-but-stable level. Continued arms sales risk triggering aggressive Chinese military posture. Background risk, no immediate flash event.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[CRITICAL\]:\*\* Hormuz partial-to-full closure -\> oil supply shock ongoing. Asset: CL (crude oil), energy equities, airline shorts, gold. Direction: Bullish CL/energy on supply shock, bearish gold short-term (rate hike risk). Date: Active, Apr 21 through ceasefire resolution.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 4. EQUITY EARNINGS  
\*\*Grade A: FactSet Earnings Insight Apr 17, 2026.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*Q1 2026 season\*\* (10% reported): 88% EPS beat rate (5Y avg: 78%). Strong start.  
\- \*\*Magnificent 7\*\*: Estimated +22.8% YoY earnings growth for Q1. NVIDIA top contributor to S\&P 500 growth.  
\- \*\*Rest of S\&P 500 (493 cos.)\*\*: +10.1% blended growth.  
\- \*\*CY 2026 full-year consensus\*\*: +18.0% EPS growth. All 11 sectors projected to show revenue growth.  
\- \*\*Apr 21 reporters\*\*: 82 companies scheduled. Volume is above-average for a single session.  
\- \*\*Sector leadership in beats\*\*: IT, Communication Services, Financials leading revenue growth expectations.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[HIGH\]:\*\* Any Mag-7 miss vs. +22.8% bar could trigger sector drawdown. Watch for guidance cuts citing supply chain disruption from Hormuz closure.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 5. CRYPTO REGULATORY  
\*\*Grade A: SEC press release, GENIUS Act text.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*SEC-CFTC token taxonomy\*\* (Mar 17, 2026): Joint interpretation clarifying federal securities law application to cryptoassets. Token categories: digital commodities, digital collectibles, digital tools, stablecoins, digital securities.  
\- \*\*GENIUS Act\*\* (stablecoin law, signed Jul 2025): Full implementation targeted 2026. Rules finalization deadline: July 18, 2026. Interest payments to holders prohibited. Stablecoin holders granted priority in insolvency.

\---

\*\*Grade A: Reuters/CNBC/WaPo primary reporting.\*\*

\#\#\# Ukraine-Russia

\#\#\# Taiwan Strait

\*\*Catalyst \[CRITICAL\]:\*\* Hormuz partial-to-full closure -\> oil supply shock ongoing. Asset: CL (crude oil), energy equities, airline shorts, gold. Direction: Bullish CL/energy on supply shock, bearish gold short-term (rate hike risk). Date: Active, Apr 21 through ceasefire resolution.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 4. EQUITY EARNINGS  
\*\*Grade A: FactSet Earnings Insight Apr 17, 2026.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*Q1 2026 season\*\* (10% reported): 88% EPS beat rate (5Y avg: 78%). Strong start.  
\- \*\*Magnificent 7\*\*: Estimated +22.8% YoY earnings growth for Q1. NVIDIA top contributor to S\&P 500 growth.  
\- \*\*Rest of S\&P 500 (493 cos.)\*\*: +10.1% blended growth.  
\- \*\*CY 2026 full-year consensus\*\*: +18.0% EPS growth. All 11 sectors projected to show revenue growth.  
\- \*\*Apr 21 reporters\*\*: 82 companies scheduled. Volume is above-average for a single session.

\---  
  
\#\# 5. CRYPTO REGULATORY  
\*\*Grade A: SEC press release, GENIUS Act text.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*SEC-CFTC token taxonomy\*\* (Mar 17, 2026): Joint interpretation clarifying federal securities law application to cryptoassets. Token categories: digital commodities, digital collectibles, digital tools, stablecoins, digital securities.  
\- \*\*GENIUS Act\*\* (stablecoin law, signed Jul 2025): Full implementation targeted 2026. Rules finalization deadline: July 18, 2026. Interest payments to holders prohibited. Stablecoin holders granted priority in insolvency.  
\- \*\*ETF landscape\*\*: Spot ETFs for BTC, ETH now under generic listing standards. SOL, XRP, LTC spot ETFs joined in late 2025. Accelerated approval window (75 days vs. prior 240 days).  
\- \*\*Apr 21 specific\*\*: No new SEC enforcement actions or ETF decisions flagged.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[MEDIUM\]:\*\* GENIUS Act July 18 rules deadline approaching — stablecoin issuers (USDC, USDT) face operational compliance changes. Watch issuer responses for BTC/ETH repricing implications.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 6. COMMODITY SUPPLY  
\*\*Grade A: IEA Oil Market Report April 2026, CNBC live pricing.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*Crude oil (CL)\*\*: Oil falling on Apr 21 amid mixed Iran peace talk messaging. Prior days: prices surged toward $115/b on Hormuz attacks. IEA forecasts 2026 peak $115/b Q2 before easing on supply restoration.  
\- \*\*OPEC+\*\*: Production fell 9.4 mb/d to 42.4 mb/d. Non-OPEC+ -770 kb/d to 54.7 mb/d.  
\- \*\*Global supply shock\*\*: Hormuz closure caused 10+ mb/d drop in Middle East output. IEA: "Largest supply disruption in history of global oil market." (Grade A — IEA primary source)  
\- \*\*Gold (XAU)\*\*: Down \~2% Apr 21, trading \~$4,800/oz. Giving back gains as renewed Hormuz hostilities drove oil higher -\> inflation concerns -\> rate hike risk weighs on gold. Gold -8%+ since Iran war began.  
\- \*\*Precious metals 2026 forecast\*\*: +5% overall; after 2025 investment-driven +40% surge.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[CRITICAL\]:\*\* CL price path hinges on Hormuz ceasefire timeline. Every failed peace-talk session = oil supply shock continuation. XAU vulnerable to further central bank tightening pricing.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 7. FX POLICY  
\*\*Grade A: Federal Reserve H.10 release Apr 20, BOJ communications.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*USD\*\*: Strong near-term on Fed hold + geopolitical safe-haven premium. 3.50-3.75% policy rate. Structural overvaluation vs. PPP and fundamental models.  
\- \*\*EUR/USD\*\*: Constructive — ECB potential rate hike narrows Fed-ECB spread, supportive for EUR. Bullish continuation signal technically.  
\- \*\*USD/JPY\*\*: Elevated — 160.00 level is Finance Ministry intervention threshold. BOJ April hike risk creates JPY upside catalyst. Bearish USD/JPY signal building.  
\- \*\*AUD\*\*: Beneficiary of commodity cycle (energy, gold); outlined as top FX pick for 2026 by OANDA/MarketPulse.  
\- \*\*FX policy watch\*\*: No G20 coordinated intervention announced. Japan verbal intervention risk elevated at 160 USD/JPY.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[HIGH\]:\*\* USD/JPY approaching 160 intervention threshold + BOJ hike risk. Asset: USD/JPY. Direction: Bearish (JPY strengthening). Date: BOJ April meeting + potential FM verbal intervention.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 8. FLASH EVENTS  
\*\*Grade A: CNBC live reporting Apr 19-21.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*Apr 19\*\*: US military destroyed Iranian tanker in escalation. Iran vowed retaliation.  
\- \*\*Apr 18\*\*: Iran re-closed Strait of Hormuz after US blockade continuation.  
\- \*\*Apr 21 (today)\*\*: Peace talks with Iran ongoing; ceasefire deadline imminent. Mixed messaging creating oil price volatility — intraday oil declining as talks progress.  
\- \*\*No equity circuit breaker events\*\* detected for Apr 21. No market-wide halt triggers.  
\- \*\*Volatility\*\*: Energy sector experiencing sharp intraday swings tied to Hormuz headlines. Last notable VIX spike: Apr 4, 2025 (+23.8 pts to 45.3) on China tariff retaliation — current energy shock could produce similar moves if talks break down.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[CRITICAL\]:\*\* Hormuz ceasefire deadline = binary event. Breakdown -\> oil spike, inflation repricing, equity sell-off. Resolution -\> CL down 10-15%, gold recovery, energy sector giveback.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 9. CREDIT / RATES  
\*\*Grade A: FRED data, Treasury DGS10 April 17, 2026.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*10Y Treasury yield\*\*: 4.26% as of April 17, 2026.  
\- \*\*IG credit spreads\*\*: \~80 bps OAS (ICE BofA Corporate Index). BBB: \~100 bps. AA: \~51 bps. AAA: \~40 bps. Near 25-year tights — last seen mid-1990s.  
\- \*\*HY spreads\*\*: Tight alongside IG; strong corporate fundamentals + global yield demand.  
\- \*\*Risk assessment\*\*: Near-historical-tight spreads indicate compressed risk premium. Limited cushion for spread widening if Hormuz crisis escalates or earnings disappoint. Rate hike risk (ECB, BOJ) could reprice duration globally.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[MEDIUM\]:\*\* 10Y at 4.26% + IG spreads near 25-year tights = limited credit buffer. Any inflation surprise from energy shock -\> spread widening catalyst. Asset: TLT/short duration, IG credit shorts. Date: Ongoing through Q2.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 10. SECTOR ROTATION  
\*\*Grade A: Morningstar, FactSet, EBC Financial Group market data.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*YTD 2026 sector performance:\*\*  
 - Energy: +22% (Exxon XOM, Chevron CVX lead)  
 - Consumer Defensive: +13.3% (Walmart WMT, Costco COST)  
 - Industrials: +16% (Caterpillar CAT lead)  
 - Technology: Underperforming — losses in mega-cap names  
 - Communication Services: Underperforming  
 - Consumer Cyclicals: Underperforming  
 - Financials: Mixed  
  
\- \*\*Rotation driver\*\*: Investors rotating from AI/tech to "real economy" stocks. Energy benefiting from Hormuz/oil shock. Industrials from AI data center buildout capex. Consumer Defensive from cost-conscious spending.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[HIGH\]:\*\* Energy sector momentum = duration of Hormuz crisis. Any ceasefire resolution triggers profit-taking in energy, rotation back to tech possible. Asset: Energy long / Tech relative short. Conditional on Hormuz resolution.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 11. ANALYST REVISIONS  
\*\*Grade B: Globe and Mail / Benzinga analyst actions.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*Upgrades\*\*: Goldman Sachs upgraded TC Energy (TRP) -\> Neutral, target US$62. International Petroleum (IPCO) raised to Outperform, target $43 (Street high). TD Cowen raised Rockpoint Gas Storage to Buy.  
\- \*\*Downgrades\*\*: BRP Inc. downgraded by National Bank to Sector Perform (was Outperform), target $80 (from $125). Advantage Energy downgraded to Market Perform, target $12.  
\- \*\*Target changes\*\*: Scotiabank raised Nutrien (NTR) to $75 (from $70). Mosaic (MOS) target lowered to $33 (from $35). CPKC raised to $125 (from $119), Outperform maintained.  
  
\*Note: Apr 21 analyst actions skew toward Canadian names in available search. US-specific Apr 21 actions not fully resolved — recommend direct Benzinga/Bloomberg check for US revisions.\*  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[LOW-MEDIUM\]:\*\* Agricultural names (Nutrien, Mosaic) diverging — fertilizer sector mixed. Energy infrastructure upgrades (TC Energy) consistent with energy sector rotation.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# 12. OVERNIGHT ASIA  
\*\*Grade A: CNBC Asia Markets Live April 21, 2026.\*\*  
  
\- \*\*Nikkei 225\*\*: +1.21%  
\- \*\*Topix\*\*: Marginally higher  
\- \*\*Hang Seng (HSI)\*\*: +0.12%  
\- \*\*CSI 300 (Shanghai)\*\*: -0.35%  
\- \*\*ASX 200\*\*: -0.28% (gave up early gains)  
\- \*\*Nifty 50 (India)\*\*: +0.43%  
\- \*\*Kospi (South Korea)\*\*: Hit record high — notable outperformer  
  
\*\*Drivers\*\*: Mixed Asia session on Iran-US peace talk hopes despite ongoing Hormuz tensions. Markets balancing ceasefire optimism vs. supply disruption reality.  
  
\*\*Notable IPO\*\*: Victory Giant (Nvidia PCB supplier) debuted HKEx +60% — largest HK IPO since Zijin Gold. Raised HK$20.1 billion (\~$2.57B USD). Signals continued investor appetite for AI supply chain names.  
  
\*\*Catalyst \[MEDIUM\]:\*\* Kospi at record high + HK IPO success = risk-on signal in Asia. CSI 300 lagging — China domestic demand concern persists. Positive Asia session provides mild tailwind to US open.  
  
\---  
  
\#\# CATALYST SUMMARY TABLE  
  
| \# | Category | Catalyst | Asset | Direction | Urgency | Grade |  
|---|----------|----------|-------|-----------|---------|-------|  
| 1 | Flash/Geo | Iran-US Hormuz ceasefire deadline — binary event | CL, XLE, airline shorts | Breakdown: bullish CL; Resolution: bearish CL | CRITICAL | A |  
| 2 | Commodity | Oil supply shock ongoing (IEA: largest ever) | CL, XLE | Bullish CL until Hormuz resolves | CRITICAL | A |  
| 3 | Central Bank | BOJ April hike risk + FM intervention at 160 | USD/JPY | Bearish USD/JPY | HIGH | A/B |  
| 4 | Earnings | 82 Q1 reporters today; Mag7 bar at +22.8% | SPY, QQQ, individual | Upside on beat, downside on Hormuz guidance cut | HIGH | A |  
| 5 | FX | ECB hawkish pivot -\> EUR/USD supportive | EUR/USD | Bullish EUR | HIGH | A |  
| 6 | Sector | Energy rotation leadership; tech lagging | XLE vs. XLK | Long energy / short tech relative | HIGH | A |  
| 7 | Credit | IG spreads at 25-yr tights; limited buffer | TLT, IG credit | Widening risk on inflation shock | MEDIUM | A |  
| 8 | Crypto | GENIUS Act rules deadline July 18 approaching | BTC, stablecoins | Compliance drag on stablecoin issuers | MEDIUM | A |  
  
\---  
  
\#\# FAIL-LOUD FLAGS  
  
\- \*\*MISSING Grade A data\*\*: Specific Apr 21 earnings company list not recovered (paywall). Recommend manual check at EarningsWhispers before open.  
\- \*\*MISSING Grade A data\*\*: Apr 21 US analyst revisions not fully captured — Canadian names dominated available search sample.  
\- \*\*Grade C exclusions\*\*: No narrative/seasonal signals used as catalyst triggers.  
  
\---  
  
\*Sources: IEA Oil Market Report April 2026; FactSet Earnings Insight April 17, 2026; CNBC Asia Markets Live April 21, 2026; Federal Reserve H.10 April 20, 2026; CNBC Hormuz/Iran coverage April 17-21, 2026; SEC Press Release 2026-30; GENIUS Act; BOJ monetary policy communications; ECB monetary policy decisions; CFR 2026 Conflict Risk Assessment.\*  "}

1	# Lessons & Corrections
2	
3	*Split from Memory.md §8 on 2026-04-16 to keep daily startup reads lean. The weekly-regime-review task writes condensed entries here; the daily-trade-rec appends one line per slot. The signal-review skill reads this file when looking for repeated patterns.*
4	
5	---
6	
7	**Week of 2026-04-14 condensed:** Workspace bootstrapped on 2026-04-14 with regime = "energy-led reflation shock + hawkish repricing, softening at margin." One trade rec issued (v8 final), 0 |Sum| ≥ 3 signals promoted, 0 trades entered, 0 violations — methodology adherence clean. EWY flagged at Sum +3 (S+1/T+1/C+1/R0) but intermediary capital fail-loud caveat blocked promotion in all 8 rec versions. Five near-misses at +2 (Cu/Au/Ag/TSM/AMZN) — blocked by R−1 crowding or T-fail-loud (residual momentum MISSING). BTC short thesis formally removed (funding negative + netflow outflow + price reclaim to $75.9k); cannot flip long due to active-address collapse (364k, −41% w/w, S = −1). Key structural decisions: added Risk Rules.md and Data Sources.md; methodology audit added 3 Grade A variables (residual momentum, intermediary capital, basis-momentum) with 6-month review gate 2026-10-14; confirmed server TZ = UTC+8; reshuffled scheduled tasks to v2 pipeline. Verbose entries archived to `archive/memory-log-2026-W16.md`.
8	- 2026-04-15 — **Workflow audit executed; 6 recommendations implemented.** (P0) Created `audit-data-compute-750pm` scheduled task (19:50 UTC+8 Mon–Fri) + Python compute script `scripts/compute_audit_additions.py` to fetch and compute the three audit-addition variables (residual momentum, intermediary capital, basis-momentum). Outputs to `audit-data-staging-YYYY-MM-DD.md` for the brief and trade-rec to consume. (P1) Updated `daily-trade-recommendation-820pm-v2` prompt with a delta-check gate: if no upstream artifact has changed since the last rec, skip the full rerun and log a one-line no-change revalidation instead. Eliminates version churn. (P2) Created `weekly-regime-review-sunday-6pm-v2` replacing v1: adds Memory §8 weekly condensation step — prior week's verbose entries rolled into a 3-5 sentence summary, originals archived to `archive/memory-log-YYYY-WNN.md`. (P3) Staggered daily cron times: brief 20:00, news 20:10, rec 20:25; all restricted to Mon–Fri. Disabled v1 weekly review. (P4) Unified retention logic: `news-events/README.md` now defers to the master `Retention Policy.md`; added explicit news-file clause to the policy. (P5) Reduced `CLAUDE.md` audit-addition section from 8 lines to a 3-line pointer to `Methodology Prompt.md` Top-25 entries 26–28. Added `audit-data-staging-YYYY-MM-DD.md` and `scripts/compute_audit_additions.py` to the CLAUDE.md file index. Updated `Data Sources.md` with automated compute pipeline references. Workflow audit report at `workflow-audit-2026-04-15.html`.
9	
10	---

# Lessons & Corrections

*Split from Memory.md §8 on 2026-04-16 to keep daily startup reads lean. The weekly-regime-review task writes condensed entries here; the daily-trade-rec appends one line per slot. The signal-review skill reads this file when looking for repeated patterns.*

---

**Week of 2026-04-14 condensed:** Workspace bootstrapped on 2026-04-14 with regime = "energy-led reflation shock + hawkish repricing, softening at margin." One trade rec issued (v8 final), 0 |Sum| ≥ 3 signals promoted, 0 trades entered, 0 violations — methodology adherence clean. EWY flagged at Sum +3 (S+1/T+1/C+1/R0) but intermediary capital fail-loud caveat blocked promotion in all 8 rec versions. Five near-misses at +2 (Cu/Au/Ag/TSM/AMZN) — blocked by R−1 crowding or T-fail-loud (residual momentum MISSING). BTC short thesis formally removed (funding negative + netflow outflow + price reclaim to $75.9k); cannot flip long due to active-address collapse (364k, −41% w/w, S = −1). Key structural decisions: added Risk Rules.md and Data Sources.md; methodology audit added 3 Grade A variables (residual momentum, intermediary capital, basis-momentum) with 6-month review gate 2026-10-14; confirmed server TZ = UTC+8; reshuffled scheduled tasks to v2 pipeline. Verbose entries archived to `archive/memory-log-2026-W16.md`.
- 2026-04-15 — **Workflow audit executed; 6 recommendations implemented.** (P0) Created `audit-data-compute-750pm` scheduled task (19:50 UTC+8 Mon–Fri) + Python compute script `scripts/compute_audit_additions.py` to fetch and compute the three audit-addition variables (residual momentum, intermediary capital, basis-momentum). Outputs to `audit-data-staging-YYYY-MM-DD.md` for the brief and trade-rec to consume. (P1) Updated `daily-trade-recommendation-820pm-v2` prompt with a delta-check gate: if no upstream artifact has changed since the last rec, skip the full rerun and log a one-line no-change revalidation instead. Eliminates version churn. (P2) Created `weekly-regime-review-sunday-6pm-v2` replacing v1: adds Memory §8 weekly condensation step — prior week's verbose entries rolled into a 3-5 sentence summary, originals archived to `archive/memory-log-YYYY-WNN.md`. (P3) Staggered daily cron times: brief 20:00, news 20:10, rec 20:25; all restricted to Mon–Fri. Disabled v1 weekly review. (P4) Unified retention logic: `news-events/README.md` now defers to the master `Retention Policy.md`; added explicit news-file clause to the policy. (P5) Reduced `CLAUDE.md` audit-addition section from 8 lines to a 3-line pointer to `Methodology Prompt.md` Top-25 entries 26–28. Added `audit-data-staging-YYYY-MM-DD.md` and `scripts/compute_audit_additions.py` to the CLAUDE.md file index. Updated `Data Sources.md` with automated compute pipeline references. Workflow audit report at `workflow-audit-2026-04-15.html`.

---

30	  1. **Gold (XAUUSDT) long OPENED 2026-04-16 22:28 UTC+8** — trailing buy fill $4,780.69, $396.80 USDT, 0.1% callback, activation ≤$4,798.34. Filled BELOW stated rec entry zone $4,800–$4,860 — trailing stop caught a dip, favorable entry vs methodology. Methodology stop $4,640 intact.
31	  2. **QQQ long OPENED in two tranches 2026-04-17 00:02 and 00:13 UTC+8** — tranche 1 trailing buy $639.04 / $198.10 USDT (activation ≤$640, 0.1% callback); tranche 2 limit buy $641.14 / $198.75 USDT. Combined ~$396.85 USDT, avg $640.09. Sum +4 signal finally executed after v3 deferral for correlation gate — equity heat now INTC (~$50) + QQQ (~$397) = $447 ≈ 11.2% of $4,000 NAV (position size); risk-at-stop combined is ~0.79% of NAV, within budget.
32	  3. **EWY long STOPPED OUT 2026-04-16 21:55:12 UTC+8** at $145.39 via reduce-only stop-market sell. Realized +$2.75 (+0.92% ROI). **Lesson: stop was too tight.** Methodology-prescribed stop was $133 (2× ATR ≈ 7.7% below entry). Actual protective stop placed at $145.39 ≈ 0.9% above entry — effectively a break-even-plus stop. This converted a Sum +3 structural thesis (DXY freefall + TSM beat) into a noise-exposed mean-reversion bet that got chopped out on normal post-catalyst sell-the-news drag (TSM ADR −3% Apr-16). Methodology requires: if protective stop is moved intraday, size must be rescaled to the new (tighter) stop-distance so risk-at-stop stays intended, OR the methodology stop must be retained. A 0.9% stop on a multi-week equity signal throws away the edge; any mean-revert tick trips it. Net: thesis was correct (EWY printed higher post-exit) but execution framework was not. This is a meaningful recurring risk to guard against on the new QQQ/Gold/INTC book, especially given Apr-17 monthly options expiry and FOMC Apr-28–29 positioning-flush potential. **Rule reinforced: do not tighten stops above entry without a documented reason AND a resize.**
33

35	  1. **P001 EWY** → Status OPEN → HIT_STOP; Exit_Price=$145.39; Exit_Date=2026-04-16; Days_to_Exit=1; Hypo_PnL_Pct=+0.90% (formula (145.39/144.1−1)×100); Notes annotated with the stop-tightening caveat. Note the per-unit Hypo_PnL (+0.90%) differs from Memory.md §7 ROI (+0.92%) because the protocol's signal-review formula is pure price, not size-weighted — this is the expected divergence and preserves comparability across the SignalLedger.
36	  2. **P004 Gold** → Taken None → YES; Entry_Price preserved at signal-time $4,840 (NOT overwritten); actual fill $4,780.69 recorded in Notes with timestamp 2026-04-16 22:28 UTC+8.
37	  3. **N019 QQQ** (near-miss) → cross-linked via Notes to new P005; Status and Taken unchanged per protocol so signal-review can still mark-to-market without double-counting.
38	  4. **P005 QQQ** → new Promoted row appended (row 29). Type=Promoted, Date=2026-04-17, Sum=+4 copied from N019, Taken=YES, Status=OPEN, Entry_Price=$640.09 (weighted avg of two tranches per Gerald's instruction), Notes document both tranches and the ad-hoc promotion reason.
39	  Reconciliation triggered creation of `trade-update.skill` and `Trade-Execution-Protocol.md` so that this class of drift cannot recur silently: every future execution event invokes the protocol's 4-layer update (Memory.md → SignalLedger → memory-lessons.md → auto-memory) in order, with fail-loud on ambiguity.

addedBlocks:
## computer-use
You have a computer-use MCP available (tools named `mcp__computer-use__*`). It lets you take screenshots of the user's desktop and control it with mouse clicks, keyboard input, and scrolling.

**Pick the right tool for the app.** Each tier trades speed/precision against coverage:

1. **Dedicated MCP for the app** — if the task is in an app that has its own MCP (Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Linear, etc.) and that MCP is connected, use it. API-backed tools are fast and precise.
2. **Chrome MCP** (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`) — if the target is a web app and there's no dedicated MCP for it, use the browser tools. DOM-aware, much faster than clicking pixels. If the Chrome extension isn't connected, ask the user to install it rather than falling through to computer use.
3. **Computer use** — for native desktop apps (Maps, Notes, Finder, Photos, System Settings, any third-party native app) and cross-app workflows. Computer use IS the right tool here — don't decline a native-app task just because there's no dedicated MCP for it.

This is about what's available, not error handling — if a dedicated MCP tool errors, debug or report it rather than silently retrying via a slower tier.

**Look before you assert.** If the user asks about app state (what's open, what's connected, what an app can do), take a screenshot and check before answering. Don't answer from memory — the user's setup or app version may differ from what you expect. If you're about to say an app doesn't support an action, that claim should be grounded in what you just saw on screen, not general knowledge. Similarly, `list_granted_applications` or a fresh `screenshot` is cheaper than a wrong assertion about what's running.

**Loading via ToolSearch — load in bulk, not one-by-one:** if computer-use tools are in the deferred list, load them ALL in a single ToolSearch call: `{ query: "computer-use", max_results: 30 }`. The keyword search matches the server-name substring in every tool name, so one query returns the entire toolkit. Don't use `select:` for individual tools — that's one round-trip per tool.

**Access flow:** before any computer-use action you must call `request_access` with the list of applications you need. The user approves each application explicitly, and you may need to call it again mid-task if you discover you need another application.

**Tiered apps:** some apps are granted at a restricted tier based on their category — the tier is displayed in the approval dialog and returned in the `request_access` response:
- **Browsers** (Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Arc, etc.) → tier **"read"**: visible in screenshots, but clicks and typing are blocked. You can read what's already on screen. For navigation, clicking, or form-filling, use the claude-in-chrome MCP (tools named `mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`; load via ToolSearch if deferred).
- **Terminals and IDEs** (Terminal, iTerm, VS Code, JetBrains, etc.) → tier **"click"**: visible and left-clickable, but typing, key presses, right-click, modifier-clicks, and drag-drop are blocked. You can click a Run button or scroll test output, but cannot type into the editor or integrated terminal, cannot right-click (the context menu has Paste), and cannot drag text onto them. For shell commands, use the Bash tool.
- **Everything else** → tier **"full"**: no restrictions.

The tier is enforced by the frontmost-app check: if a tier-"read" app is in front, `left_click` returns an error; if a tier-"click" app is in front, `type` and `right_click` return errors. The error tells you what tier the app has and what to do instead. `open_application` works at any tier — bringing an app forward is a read-level operation.

**Link safety — treat links in emails and messages as suspicious by default.**
- **Never click web links with computer-use tools.** If you encounter a link in a native app (Mail, Messages, a PDF, etc.), do NOT `left_click` it. Open the URL via the claude-in-chrome MCP instead.
- **See the full URL before following any link.** Visible link text can be misleading — hover or inspect to get the real destination.
- **Links from emails, messages, or unknown-sender documents are suspicious by default.** If the destination URL is at all unfamiliar or looks off, ask the user for confirmation before proceeding.
- **Inside the Chrome extension** you can click links with the extension's tools, but the suspicion check still applies — verify unfamiliar URLs with the user.

**Financial actions - do not execute trades or move money.** Budgeting and accounting apps (Quicken, YNAB, QuickBooks, etc.) are granted at full tier so you can categorize transactions, generate reports, and help the user organize their finances. But never execute a trade, place an order, send money, or initiate a transfer on the user's behalf - always ask the user to perform those actions themselves.

The trade-rec was **not uploaded to Google Drive**.

The GDrive upload was skipped during the cloud-7pm run because the base64-encoded content of `trade-rec-2026-04-21.md` (~33.2KB) exceeded what the MCP tool could handle in context. This was explicitly noted in the Slack post to #trading-scheduled-updates: *"GDrive upload: SKIPPED (content too large for cloud tool context — file is local only)."*

This was then independently confirmed by the local 20:20 UTC+8 run, which searched Drive for the cloud trade-rec and returned: *"Cloud trade-rec-2026-04-21.md: CLOUD MISSING (not found on Drive)"* — the local run uses the Drive presence as a v1 baseline check, and it found nothing.

The authoritative file is local only: [`Trade/2026-04-21/trade-rec-2026-04-21.md`](Trade/2026-04-21/trade-rec-2026-04-21.md) (v2 Local Authoritative, 20:20 UTC+8).
